operating_state: Disney faced significant operational headwinds during Q2 2023, with traditional linear viewership declining industry-wide and reducing advertising revenue across broadcast networks. The company continued experiencing losses in its direct-to-consumer streaming services while forgoing revenue from traditional distribution channels as it expanded DTC offerings. Theater attendance remained below pre-COVID levels, impacting theatrical revenue streams.
cost_structure: Content impairment charges of $1.5 billion were recorded in Q3 with additional charges up to $0.4 billion anticipated as Disney removed produced content from DTC platforms during strategic content curation review. Operating expenses increased due to COVID-19 protection costs and compliance investments for expanding environmental, social and governance reporting requirements. Labor cost pressures emerged from collective bargaining renegotiations and the Writers Guild work stoppage that commenced May 2, 2023.
material_events: Christine McCarthy took leave of absence as CFO effective July 1, 2023, with Kevin Lansberry appointed as Interim CFO. Disney redeemed $201.2 million in senior debentures and $6.4 million in floating rate notes during the quarter. The company extended Chief Communications Officer Kristina Schake's employment agreement through 2026 with increased compensation reflecting expanded responsibilities.
macro_exposures: Florida legislative actions specifically targeting Disney threatened to impact business strategy execution and operational profitability. The Writers Guild work stoppage beginning May 2023 created production disruption risks for programming and theatrical releases. Generative AI regulatory uncertainty presented new intellectual property protection challenges and potential revenue stream impacts.
forward_outlook: Management signaled continued DTC expansion despite ongoing losses, with plans to reorganize media and entertainment businesses to advance streaming strategies. The company anticipates further content removals from platforms through Q3 as part of strategic content curation. Disney expects to forgo additional traditional revenue sources as DTC offerings expand, with no assurance the streaming model will achieve profitability levels of historic business models.
uncertainty_profile: Early Disney copyrights including Steamboat Willie expire at end of 95-year terms, potentially reducing IP-derived revenues. Prolonged labor disputes could prevent production and distribution of programming and theatrical content. Environmental sustainability goal achievement depends on third-party collaboration and availability of economically feasible solutions at scale, with stakeholder perception risks to reputation and brands.
